method: Sonnet subagent re-review of raw coupon files, 3 parallel batches

items.rationale: 'GO Pass admits two adults and children under 18 at the family membership admission price' — per-audience counts present (Adult=2, Child unlimited); family membership rate is variable so no single discount value applies.
'Pass is for free admission for a family to Bedrock Gardens' — 'family' is a variable-size unit with no fixed headcount.
'Pass will admit 2 adults and children under 18' — per-audience counts are present (Adult=2, Child unlimited); no single numeric discount value is stated.
'20% discount for online tickets' gives percent-off value (already captured correctly) but states no headcount limit, so capacity=unspecified is correct as-is.
'Pass admits 2 adults (children under 18 are free)' — Adult count=2 is explicit per-audience; no specific percent/dollar value for adult admission is stated.
'Free admission to Trustees properties… apply the coupon discount on any date' — no headcount and no specific discount value is given in the text.
'Reduced admission for 2 adults and 2 children (under 17 yrs of age)' — both audience counts are explicit (Adult=2, Child=2); the exact reduced rate varies by property.
'Free admission for 2 adults and children under the age of 18' — Adult=2 explicit, children count unbounded; per-audience breakdown captures this correctly.
'GO pass admits 2 adults and any children under the age of 18' — Adult count=2 is explicit; discount rates vary by property so no fixed value applies.
'Half-price Seacoast Science Center admission' — 50% is already captured; no headcount limit stated so capacity=unspecified is correct.
'Admits two adults and children under 18 for free' — Adult=2 count explicit; children count is unbounded; per-audience structure is correct.
Raw text is a venue description only; no discount amount, percentage, or headcount is mentioned.
Raw text is entirely a venue/tour description with no discount value or headcount stated.
'Up to two tickets at the discounted Library Pass rate' — capacity=ticket/2 is already correct; no specific dollar or percent value is given for the 'Library Pass rate'.
'Coupon code good for one boat rental per order' — a rental count is implied but no dollar value or percent off is stated, and no passes are currently available.
Raw text is purely a venue description with no discount value, percentage, or headcount mentioned.
'Two adults and children under 18 entry at Family membership rates' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family membership rate is variable per property.
'Use the code on your confirmation email to redeem your Community Pass ticket(s)' — no headcount limit and no discount value specified; form=free but count is unbounded.
'50% off all Boston Harbor Islands Ferry tickets' — percent-off/50 already correctly captured; no headcount limit is stated so capacity=unspecified is correct.
Raw text is a venue description only with 'No passes are available at this time'; no discount value or headcount is mentioned.
'Admits up to 2 adults and children (under 18) at free or reduced admission' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; per-audience structure is correct.

'One adult 50% discounted admission / Children (ages 5-17) $1.00 admission' — both audience rows already correctly extracted (Adult count=1 percent-off/50, Child 5-17 per-person-price/$1); capacity is fully expressed per-audience.
'Admits two people at a discounted price' — capacity=people/2 is already correct; no specific percent or dollar value for the discount is given.
'Two adults and children under 18' at 'Trustees Family Level Membership' rate — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family membership value varies by property.
'Each pass admits 2 adults & children under 18 at the Family Level admission rate' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family-level rate varies by property.
'Up to 2 people at the discounted library price per person' — capacity=people/2 already correct; no specific dollar or percent value for the library price is given.
'50% discount on admission for each visitor… aged 13 and over' — percent-off/50 already captured; no per-pass headcount limit, capacity=unspecified is correct.
'Up to 6 people for a discounted price' — capacity=people/6 already correct; no specific percent or dollar discount value is stated.
'GO Pass coupon admits 2 adults and children under 18 to Trustees properties' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; admission equivalent to family membership varies per property.
'Two adults & children 17 and under' at family-level rate — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family membership price varies per property.
'50% discount for the 202 Boston Harbor Ferry season' — percent-off/50 already captured; no per-pass headcount limit stated so capacity=unspecified is correct.
'Free Lecture Series Pass… present it upon entrance' — form=free is correct but no headcount is stated so capacity=unspecified is correct.
'50% discount on the cardholder's admission ticket… aged 13 and over' — percent-off/50 already captured; no per-pass headcount limit so capacity=unspecified is correct.
'GoPass is good for admission of two adults and all children under the age of 18' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family membership rate varies per property.
'Trustees GOPASS admits 2 adults & children under 18 at the Family Level admission rate' — Adult=2 explicit, children unbounded; family-level rate varies by location.
Raw text distinguishes 'visitors aged 13 and over' (50% off) from 'visitors aged 12 and under' (free admission) — per-audience counts are genuinely split, no single global n applies.
Raw text says 'one family/same household per day' — household size is variable by definition, no bounded person count.

Raw text specifies 'two adults and children under 18' — adult count=2 is fixed but child count is unbounded; form=discount/value=null is correct because Trustees charge 'Family Membership admission price' which varies by property.
Raw text distinguishes '50% discount' for visitors aged 13+ from implied free for aged 12 and under — two distinct audience tiers with no unified group cap.
